explanation: Instruct the robot to locate the target object and move to its vicinity. If the object cannot be found, return an error message.

action: pick(obj_name: str, obj_num: Optional[int], manualInteract: bool = False) -> str
explanation: Move to the target object, close the gripper, and attempt to grasp it. Returns an error message when object lookup or grasp preconditions fail.
Move the held object above a receptacle and release it. Requires the robot to already hold an object.
Translate end-effector left in Cartesian space by a small fixed delta while preserving grasp state.
Translate end-effector right in Cartesian space by a small fixed delta while preserving grasp state.
Translate end-effector forward in Cartesian space by a small fixed delta while preserving grasp state.
Translate end-effector backward in Cartesian space by a small fixed delta while preserving grasp state.
Mark target object as open in symbolic state after locating it.
Mark target object as closed in symbolic state after locating it.
Mark target object as sliced in symbolic state after locating it.
Mark target object as switched on in symbolic state after locating it.
Mark target object as switched off in symbolic state after locating it.
Open gripper to release currently held object at current end-effector pose.
Move the hand forward and release the held object to simulate a throw.
Mark target object as broken in symbolic state.
Mark target object as cooked in symbolic state.
Mark target object as dirty in symbolic state.
Mark target object as clean in symbolic state.

action: fillLiquid(obj_name: str, obj_num: Optional[int], liquid_name: str) -> str
explanation: Mark target object as filled with a specific liquid in symbolic state.
Clear filled liquid state for the target object.
Execute a short pouring wrist trajectory and clear liquid state of held object.
Insert a currently held object into a target receptacle/slot using a precise downward motion.
Rotate end-effector yaw to the left while preserving grasp state.
Rotate end-effector yaw to the right while preserving grasp state.
Rotate end-effector pitch upward while preserving grasp state.
Rotate end-effector pitch downward while preserving grasp state.

action: push(obj_name: str, direction: str, obj_num: Optional[int]) -> str
explanation: Approach the target object from the opposite side of the push direction, then use low-gain compliant motion to push it. Direction must be one of: left, right, forward, back. The robot does NOT grasp the object; it uses its end-effector to apply contact force.

action: pull(obj_name: str, direction: str, obj_num: Optional[int]) -> str
explanation: Grasp the target object, then use low-gain compliant motion to drag it in the specified direction. Direction must be one of: left, right, forward, back. After pulling, the robot releases the object.

action: nudge(obj_name: str, direction: str, obj_num: Optional[int]) -> str
explanation: Give the target object a short, gentle tap in the specified direction using low-gain compliant contact. Useful for fine adjustments. Direction must be one of: left, right, forward, back.
